home.warnDetail: Plug the mixer in via USB before Sunday
home.readyNoSchedule: Ready — set up a schedule to start recordings automatically
home.testBusy: Can't run a test while a recording is in progress.
home.testSignalLow: ⚠️ Weak signal — check gain on the mixer
home.videoPreviewPrompt: Choose a camera and click Refresh

banner.more: more — see Settings → Audio → Run system check

update.downloaded: Version {v} ready — will install on next restart.
update.checkHint: Click “Check for updates” to check now
update.notBuilt: Auto-update isn't built into this version.

history.title: History
history.subtitle: All recordings from the past 6 months
history.revealError: Could not open the folder — the file may no longer exist

review.bannerDetail: We've detected the sermon and suggested a trim. Look it over and click publish.
review.bannerNoSermon: We didn't find a clear sermon block. Check the file before publishing.
review.discardConfirm: Discard this episode? The recording itself is kept, but it won't be published.
review.detectedSermon: We detected a {min}-min sermon and suggested a trim. Review and click publish.
review.noSermonFound: No clear sermon block found. Review the file before publishing.
review.title: Review
review.loadError: Could not read the review queue

schedule.title: Weekly schedule
schedule.subtitle: Fixed recordings that repeat automatically every week.
schedule.wakeDesc: Starts automatically a few minutes before the recording begins
schedule.wakeStatusPermission: Missing permission — click Schedule and approve
schedule.macStep2: Paste this command and press Enter:
schedule.macStep3: This stops your Mac from powering off completely after sleep. Enter your admin password when prompted.
schedule.winStep2: Click Change plan settings → Change advanced power settings
schedule.winStep4: Or: open PowerShell as administrator and run:
schedule.winStep5: This disables hibernate and keeps regular sleep (S3), which can always be woken.
schedule.protectTitle: Protect ongoing recordings from accidental stop
schedule.protectDesc: Requires confirmation before stopping an ongoing recording
schedule.wakeCannotOff: Cannot start from fully powered off
schedule.wakeTipNote: Tip: leave the computer in sleep (don't shut it down) when you head home on Saturday evening. Macs won't wake on battery — keep the power adapter plugged in.
schedule.silenceDesc: Ends the recording if the audio stays silent for more than 5 minutes
schedule.splitDesc: Creates a new audio file every hour — easier to edit afterwards
schedule.errTimes: Start and stop time can't be the same.
schedule.noSlotsTitle: No weekly recordings set up yet
schedule.noSlotsHint: Click “Add a time” below to start a recurring weekly recording — e.g. Sunday at 11.
schedule.sleepFixNoAdmin: Needs administrator rights — run SundayRec as administrator
schedule.reminderDesc: System notification N minutes before a scheduled recording starts
schedule.manualMaxDesc: Stops automatically after the set time — keeps files from getting huge if you forget to stop
schedule.preRollDesc: Captures the last N seconds before you pressed record — catches the start even if you're a touch late
schedule.sleepStatusOk: Sleep settings look good — wake-up will work
schedule.sleepStatusMacWarn: Autopoweroff is on — your Mac may shut down during sleep
schedule.sleepStatusWinWarn: Wake timers aren't enabled — wake-up may fail
schedule.sleepFixOk: Fixed — sleep settings are now optimal
schedule.sleepFixCancelled: Cancelled — approve the permission prompt to fix
schedule.sleepAlwaysOffNote: Want the machine to always start recordings? Leave it in sleep (don't shut it down) and turn on “Start with Windows/Mac” under General.
schedule.wakeMainDesc: Wakes the computer and starts recording on its own — even when the machine is asleep
schedule.platformMacArm: Apple Silicon Mac (M-series) — can wake from sleep, not from shutdown
schedule.platformMacIntel: Intel Mac — can wake from sleep and shutdown (manual activation required for the latter)
schedule.platformWin: Windows — can wake from sleep; cold-start from full shutdown requires BIOS configuration
schedule.platformLinux: Linux — automatic wake is not supported
schedule.platformOther: Platform not supported for automatic wake

capability.appleSilicon: Apple Silicon Mac (M-series) — can wake from sleep, not from a full shutdown
capability.intelMac: Intel Mac — can wake from sleep; starting from shutdown requires manual setup
capability.windows: Windows — can wake from sleep; starting from full shutdown requires BIOS configuration
capability.linux: Linux — automatic wake is not supported

power.onBattery: On battery — wake will likely fail.

standby.warning: Standby (deep sleep) is enabled — it can break wake on Apple Silicon.

verify.checking: Checking what's actually scheduled in the OS…
verify.none: No wake jobs expected — you have no upcoming recordings.
verify.mismatch: confirmed in the OS — some are missing. Click “Schedule wake-up” to set them again.

test.confirm:
The computer will sleep for up to 60 seconds and then try to wake itself.

Close any unsaved work first. Continue?

battery.warning: The computer is on battery — wake will likely fail.

bios.info: If the test wake fails on Windows, “Wake on RTC” must be enabled in the BIOS. It cannot be enabled from software.

calendar.title: Church Calendar
calendar.subtitle: Schedule recordings for special days like Christmas Eve, Easter and other holy days.
calendar.autofillDesc: The app knows the Norwegian church calendar and can suggest names and dates automatically.
calendar.newSpecialSub: Schedule a single recording for a specific date.
calendar.name: NAME (suggestion from the church calendar)
calendar.errNoDate: Pick a date in the calendar.
calendar.hintShort: Click a day to add a recording
calendar.errPastDate: The date is already in the past — the recording won't start

audio.title: Audio Source
audio.subtitle: Choose the USB mixer to record from
audio.inputVolumeHint: 100% = no change to the signal. For the best audio quality, adjust the level on the mixer itself.
audio.sampleRateHint: 48,000 Hz is standard for video and church audio
audio.testDesc: Listen to the selected source in real time through your speakers.
audio.inputChannelsDesc: Choose which channels from the audio interface to record. Useful for multi-channel mixers (X32, SQ, TF, etc.).
audio.channelHint: Tip: to record mono from a single channel, pick the same channel for L and R, then set Channels to Mono below.
audio.compatDesc: USB mixers work on Mac without extra drivers. On Windows, the manufacturer's driver is recommended to access all channels. ASIO mode doesn't work — use WDM/WASAPI.
audio.compatAsio: Windows: ASIO mode isn't reachable from the browser API. Pick WDM or WASAPI in the driver settings.
audio.compressorDesc: Evens out volume swings in the recording
audio.limiterDesc: Stops clipping at high signal levels (recommended)

files.title: Files & Format
files.subtitle: Choose where and how recordings are saved
files.smartNameDesc: The app recognises church holidays and uses the correct name automatically.
files.autoDeleteDesc: Frees up disk space automatically by removing old recordings
files.trimSilenceDesc: Automatically trims silent seconds at the start and end of the recording
files.confirmAutoDeleteShort: Recordings older than {n} days will be deleted automatically and can't be recovered. Are you sure?
files.behaviourDesc: Fine-tune how the recording starts, is protected, and split.

general.title: General
general.langDetected: Detected from the operating system: English. You can change it below.
general.langHint: The change takes effect after you click Save.
general.emailErrorDesc: Sends an email to the person in charge if the recording fails
general.autoStartDesc: Runs quietly in the background — nothing for you to do
general.showOnStartupDesc: Opens the window automatically when the app starts. Otherwise it stays quietly in the system tray.
general.autoUpdateDesc: Downloads and installs new versions quietly in the background
general.testEmailNoConfig: Please fill in the SMTP settings
general.askOpenEditorDesc: Show a prompt to open the file in the Editor once a recording finishes
general.notifyStartDesc: Shown as a system notification when a scheduled session begins.
general.notifyStopDesc: Click the notification to jump straight to the file.
general.diagnoseHint: Copy this and send to support if recording is not working.

editor.title: Edit audio file
editor.emptyDesc: Pick an audio file to start editing. You can trim the start and end, or cut sections out of the middle.
editor.dragHint: Click and drag to mark a cut
editor.saveConfirm: Choose whether to save as a new file alongside the original, or replace the original file.
editor.normalizeHint: Adjusts the peak to -1 dBFS for a safe final mix.
editor.normalizeResult: Peak now at -1 dBFS — safe for export.
editor.normalizeAlready: The peak is already at -1 dBFS — no change needed.
editor.emptyTitleDrop: Drop a file here — or click to open
editor.markSermonHint: Trims everything before and after the highlighted sermon block.
editor.ioDragHint: — or drag a file onto the timeline
editor.exportPublishPhase2: Coming in a later version — requires separate OAuth setup
editor.confirmClose: You have unsaved changes. Close anyway?
editor.confirmOpenOther: You have unsaved changes. Open a new file anyway?
editor.videoFileNotice: Video file — exported as MP4 (H.264 + AAC)
editor.errReplaceUnsafe: Cannot overwrite the original file in this format. Use "Save as new file" instead.
editor.errNoAudioRemaining: No audio left — the cuts cover the entire recording. Remove at least one cut before exporting.
editor.errTimeout: Export took too long and was stopped. Try again, or split the file into smaller recordings.
editor.errFileNotFound: Original file is not available — is the disk disconnected?
editor.errCutData: Internal error in cut data. Try reloading the file.
editor.chaptersEmpty: No chapters yet. Click "+ Add at playhead" to start.
editor.featureDisabled: Editing is not built into this version.

master.title: Mastering (prepare for publishing)
master.intro: Standardizes loudness and audio quality for podcast and streaming. Uses two-pass EBU R128 normalization.

manual.title: Start manual recording
manual.subtitle: Recording starts straight away and you stop it yourself when you're done.

recording.confirmBody: The recording will be saved up to this point.
recording.disconnected: The audio source was disconnected during recording
recording.errorPermission: Microphone access denied — open System Settings and grant access
recording.errorDeviceNotFound: Audio device not found — check the USB connection
recording.errorOverconstrained: The audio device doesn't support the selected settings
recording.errorNotReadable: The audio device is in use by another application
recording.errorDeviceError: Error opening the audio device — try reconnecting it
recording.errorAlreadyRecording: A recording is already in progress
recording.errorEmpty: The recording is empty — no audio reached the app from the device
recording.errorFolderPermission: No access to the save folder — make sure it's writable
recording.errorFolderError: Can't create the save folder — check disk space and permissions
recording.errorDeviceDisconnected: The audio device was disconnected during recording — check the connection
recording.errorDiskFull: The disk is full — free up space and try again
recording.errorFfmpegMissing: Internal error: the recording binary is missing — reinstall the app
recording.errorStuck: Recording stopped — no audio from the device for 60 seconds

video.title: Video recording
video.enableDesc: Captures video alongside audio for every recording
video.cameraSearchPrompt: Click Refresh to search for cameras
video.cameraHint: USB webcams and HDMI capture cards are supported on macOS and Windows.
video.outputFormatDesc: Choose whether audio and video are combined in one file or saved separately.
video.combinedDesc: Audio + video in one file — ready for YouTube
video.separateDesc: Audio and video are saved separately
video.splitWarning: File splitting is active — video is always saved as a separate file regardless of the choice above. Disable automatic splitting in schedule settings to combine audio and video into one MP4.
video.splitHint: When automatic file splitting is enabled, separate files are always used.
video.keepAudioDesc: Also saves the high-quality audio file alongside the combined MP4
video.unifiedDesc: Uses a single ffmpeg process for both audio and video — eliminates all sync drift. Recommended on. Turn off only if you experience unexpected video recording issues.

wizard.q1: Which audio mixer do you use?
wizard.q1sub: Pick the device connected via USB.
wizard.q2sub: The app starts the recording on its own — no one needs to do anything.
wizard.q3: Where should the recordings be saved?
wizard.q3sub: Pick a folder that's easy to find.
wizard.usbHint: Plug in the USB mixer if it doesn't show up here
wizard.title: Setup
wizard.welcomeBody: Let's set up the app so everything is ready for Sunday.
wizard.deviceTitle: Which audio device do you use?
wizard.deviceBody: Pick the mixer or audio interface connected to this computer.
wizard.pickDeviceFirst: Choose an audio device before continuing
wizard.testBody: Say something into the microphone — check that the meter moves.
wizard.readyBody: SundayRec is ready to record services. You can change any setting from the menu at any time.

notify.smtpPassHint: Gmail requires an "App password" from your account security settings.
notify.webhookIntro: Send notifications to a chat channel in addition to email. Paste the webhook URL from Slack, Discord or Teams.
notify.webhookOnWarnDesc: By default only error messages are sent. Enable this to also be notified about minor issues.
notify.emailGmailDesc: Send via your Gmail account — no SMTP setup required.
notify.emailGmailConfirmDisconnect: Disconnect Google account? Email notifications will fall back to SMTP.

prep.sectionDesc: After each recording it is automatically analysed (sermon detection), prepped with mastering and intro/outro, and queued for human review before publishing.
prep.enableDesc: Run analysis + prep after each recording
prep.howto: Getting started: Upload 1-3 test recordings to Google Drive, click "Generate feed now", and send the resulting URL to Spotify for Podcasters and Apple Podcasts Connect. After approval (1-2 days), every new recording appears automatically.

tooltip.jumpToClip: Click to jump to first clip

publish.descPlaceholder: E.g. Services from Example Church, every Sunday at 11
publish.podcastIntro: Automatically generates an RSS feed after each recording. Send the feed URL once to Spotify for Podcasters and Apple Podcasts Connect — new services appear by themselves.
publish.feedUrlLabel: FEED URL — send this to Spotify and Apple Podcasts
publish.errConnectFirst: connect to the cloud service first
publish.errNotConfigured: is not configured in this build. Ask the developer for a build with an OAuth key.
publish.needsReauth: needs sign-in again. Click to reconnect.
publish.oauthKeyMissing: OAuth key is not set in this build.
publish.streamIntro: Stream services live to YouTube, Facebook or your own RTMP server.
publish.streamNoneYet: No destinations yet. Add one to start streaming.
publish.title: Publishing
publish.featureDisabled: Publishing to disk/cloud is not built into this version. You can still preview the feed.
publish.noFolder: Choose a save folder in settings first.

live.title: Live broadcast
live.subtitle: Stream to YouTube, Facebook or your own RTMP server
live.noDestinations: No destinations configured. Go to Settings → Publishing.
live.previewWaiting: Preview appears when the stream starts
live.errNoActive: Enable at least one destination before starting.
live.alsoRecordHint: Local recording lands in Recent recordings when the stream stops — higher bitrate than the livestream, ready for editing or podcast.
live.overlaysDesc: Add the church logo, lyrics from EasyWorship (via screen capture on the same machine), or other graphics on top of what is being streamed. Overlays only affect the livestream — recordings stay clean.

transcript.title: Transcription
transcript.empty: No transcription yet. Click "Transcribe" to create searchable text from the speech.
transcript.modalDesc: Uses local AI (whisper.cpp) — no data sent to the cloud. Choose model and language.
transcript.translateToEn: Translate to English at the same time
transcript.unavailableHint: Transcription is not available in this build. Check Settings → System for platform info.
transcript.errNoBinary: Whisper is not available on this platform. Contact support.

search.title: Search sermons
search.subtitle: Search across all transcribed recordings. Click a hit to open the recording at that point.
search.noTranscriptsDesc: Open a recording in the Editor and click Transcribe to build a searchable archive. Once you have one or more transcripts they will appear here.
search.fileNotFound: Source file not found. It may have been moved or deleted.
search.empty: No transcripts yet — transcribe a recording first.

default.title: Default episode artwork
default.subtitle: Used as cover art for every sermon unless you override it per episode.

thumbnail.dropHint: Drop an image here, or click to choose

warning.tooSmall: Recommended size is at least 1400×1400 pixels
warning.tooLarge: Files over 5 MB may slow recording and upload

error.unsupported: Unsupported image format. Use JPG, PNG or WebP.

email.title: Email notifications
email.gmailHint: Connect Google under Cloud backup to send via Gmail without SMTP.
email.testFailed: Could not send the test notification.
email.noConfig: Fill in a recipient and email setup.
email.featureDisabled: Email notifications are not built into this version. Settings can still be saved.

transcribe.title: Transcription
transcribe.featureDisabled: Transcription is not built into this version.
transcribe.empty: No speech found in the recording.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.handoffHint: Send a finished recording to SundayEdit or SundayStudio from History.
integrations.bridgeDisabled: The built-in live bridge is not compiled into this version. You can still save the connection and test the channel name.
integrations.channelError: Fill in both church ID and service ID.

editScreen.loudnessMeasured: Measured {{input}} LUFS → target {{target}} LUFS · peak {{peak}} dBTP.
editScreen.normalizeDescAudio: Adjusts the peak to −1 dBFS for a safe final mix.
editScreen.masteringDesc: Standardizes loudness and audio quality for podcasts and streaming. Uses EBU R128 normalization.
editScreen.loudnessMeasuredVideo: Measured {{input}} LUFS → target {{target}} LUFS. The picture is untouched.
editScreen.normalizeDescVideo: Adjusts the video's audio to −1 dBFS. The picture is untouched.
editScreen.trimDesc: Cut away dead time before and after the service. Trimming applies to both picture and audio at once.
editScreen.exportVideoDesc: With video loaded you can export a finished MP4 for YouTube, or extract just the audio for podcast.
editScreen.trimDescDrag: Drag the gold handles on the waveform to set start and end — or type the times below. Trim applies to both picture and sound.

homeScreen.audioOnlyWav: Recording will be audio only · WAV
homeScreen.separateAudioSub: In addition to the video file, a separate audio file is saved in the chosen format.

liveScreen.title: Live stream
liveScreen.subtitle: Stream to YouTube, Facebook or your own RTMP server.
liveScreen.previewPlaceholder: video preview · what is being streamed
liveScreen.overlaysDesc: Add the church logo, song lyrics or other graphics over the broadcast. Affects the stream only — the recording stays clean.
liveScreen.startHelpText: The local recording lands in “Recent recordings” when the stream stops — higher quality than the live stream.

scheduleScreen.pageSubtitle: Recurring recordings that repeat automatically — the machine starts and stops by itself, even when no one is present.
scheduleScreen.clickDayHint: Click a day to add a recording
scheduleScreen.weeklyDesc: Recurring recordings that repeat every week — for example a service every Sunday.
scheduleScreen.noWeeklySlots: No weekly recordings yet. Add a time slot below.
scheduleScreen.wakeDesc: Lets the machine wake in time for recordings and verifies that it works.

confidence.chipNeedsOn: Requires the machine to stay on
confidence.wakeReady: The machine will wake automatically at {{wakeAt}} and start recording at {{recAt}}.
confidence.wakeNeedsOn: The machine must stay ON (not asleep) for the recording to start.
confidence.wakeCaveatCan: Turn on automatic wake, and keep the machine plugged into power and only asleep (not fully shut down). The lid may be closed.
confidence.wakeCaveatCannot: This machine cannot be woken automatically. Leave it on and plugged into power until the recording.

searchScreen.title: Search sermons
searchScreen.subtitle: Search across all transcribed recordings. Click a result to open the recording at that point.
searchScreen.minQuery: Type at least two characters to search the transcripts.
searchScreen.emptyNoTranscripts: No transcripts yet — open a recording in Edit and click Transcribe to build a searchable archive.
searchScreen.emptyNoRecordings: No transcripts yet — transcribe a recording first.

settingsScreen.pageSubtitle: Everything advanced lives here — gathered, centered and calm.

audio.devicesDesc: Choose the mixer or audio interface that captures the sound in the church. A USB mixer is recommended over the built-in microphone.
audio.deviceHelp: Can't see the right device? Check that the audio interface is connected.
audio.checkDesc: Run these before a service to make sure the recording starts without problems.
audio.processingDesc: Optional. Off by default — a raw recording gives the most flexibility afterwards.
audio.eqDesc: Light tonal adjustment on the way in.
audio.compDesc: Evens out fluctuations in voice level.
audio.limiterDesc: Prevents sudden loud sounds from distorting.
audio.noDevicesFound: No audio devices found. Connect your audio interface and press Refresh.
audio.showLevelsDesc: The L/R meter shows while recording. Turn off for maximum capture stability on a busy machine.
audio.processingMoved: Audio processing (EQ, compressor, limiter) now happens in editing — the recording is always raw.

video.enableDesc: Records video in addition to audio for every recording.
video.cameraHelp: USB webcams and HDMI capture cards are supported on macOS and Windows.
video.outputDesc: Choose whether audio and video are combined into one file or saved separately.
video.combinedSub: Audio + video in one file — ready for YouTube
video.separateSub: Audio and video are saved separately
video.keepAudioDesc: Also saves the high-quality audio file alongside the MP4.
video.avSyncDesc: Uses a single ffmpeg process for both audio and video — eliminates sync drift.

files.folderDesc: All recordings end up here as local files.
files.patternDesc: The app recognizes church holidays and uses the correct name automatically.
files.behaviorDesc: Fine-tune how the recording starts, is protected and split.
files.autoDeleteDesc: Frees up disk space by deleting recordings older than 90 days.
files.protectDesc: Requires confirmation to stop an ongoing recording.
files.stopSilenceDesc: Ends the recording if the audio is silent for more than 5 minutes.
files.preRollDesc: Starts the recording a few seconds back in time — captures the beginning even if you press a little too late.
files.splitDesc: Creates a new file every hour — easier to edit afterwards.

publishing.heroDesc: Set up automatic cloud backup and — if you wish — a podcast on Spotify and Apple Podcasts. Everything is optional.
publishing.coverDesc: Used as cover art for all sermons unless you override it per episode.
publishing.cloudDesc: Upload recordings automatically to Google Drive — extra safety and easy sharing.
publishing.streamDesc: Stream services live to YouTube, Facebook or your own RTMP server.
publishing.podcastDesc: Generates an RSS feed automatically after each recording. Submit the feed URL once to Spotify and Apple Podcasts.

notifications.onStartDesc: Shown as a system notification when a scheduled session begins.
notifications.onStopDesc: Click the notification to go straight to the file.
notifications.reminderDesc: System notification N minutes before a scheduled recording starts.
notifications.emailOnErrorDesc: Sends an email to the responsible person if the recording fails.
notifications.webhookDesc: Send notifications to a chat channel in addition to email.
notifications.webhookWarningDesc: By default, only error messages are sent.

system.languageDesc: SundayRec supports seven languages — all menus and notifications adapt immediately.
system.churchProfileDesc: Used in filenames, notification emails and the podcast RSS.
system.launchDesc: Runs quietly in the background — no action needed.
system.showOnStartupDesc: Opens the window automatically. Otherwise it starts quietly in the system tray.
system.askEditorDesc: Suggests opening the file in Edit when the recording is finished.
system.autoUpdateDesc: Downloads and installs new versions quietly in the background.

suite.introDesc: Connect SundayRec to the sister apps in the Sunday suite. Everything is optional and off by default — SundayRec works exactly as before without it.
suite.enableDesc: Master switch. When off, no integration code runs.
suite.verbatimDesc: Send a video recording to Verbatim for professional captioning; it comes back as a transcript.
suite.songDesc: Send the song list to SundaySong for automatic license reports.
suite.planDesc: Fetches upcoming services and fills in title and speaker automatically.
suite.stageDesc: Import Stage's cue log to set chapter markers automatically.
suite.connectionDesc: Shared fields used by the Song and Plan integrations.
